(“VMware”). Over the years, we have assembled a large team of semiconductor and software design engineers around the world. payload.thought:
I now have comprehensive coverage across all required dimensions. Let me compile the key findings:

**Filings covered (6 most recent SEC filings before 2026-02-13):**
1. 8-K filed 2025-09-10 (Q3 FY2025 earnings + VMware pro forma)
2. 8-K filed 2025-09-29 (debt/notes offering)
3. 10-Q filed 2025-09-10 (quarterly report ended Aug 3, 2025)
4. 8-K filed 2025-12-11 (Q4/FY2025 earnings + dividend)
5. 10-K filed 2025-12-18 (FY2025 annual report)
6. 8-K filed 2026-01-13 (senior notes offering)

**Key data points gathered:**
- Q4 FY2025 results: Revenue $18.0B (+28% YoY), AI semi revenue +74% YoY
- Q1 FY2026 guidance: Revenue ~$19.1B, AI semi revenue $8.2B (doubling YoY), Adjusted EBITDA 67%
- FY2025: Revenue $63.9B, Adj EBITDA $43.0B (+35%), FCF $26.9B
- Segment: Semi solutions $36.9B (+22%), Infrastructure software $27.0B (+26%)
- Debt: ~$62.8B long-term debt (as of Q3), new notes offering Jan 2026
- Risk factors: customer concentration, cyclicality, contract manufacturing dependence, trade tensions
- Price: $323.98 as of 2/13, down from ~$350 in late Dec, -4.13% over 60-day window
